expectations.text: Uses a switch statement on the RejectionReason parameter in didReject
expectations.evidence: Lines 86–95: `switch reason { case .documentExpired: ... case .holderUnderage: ... case .timeout: ... default: ... }`

expectations.text: Has a case for .documentExpired with a distinct user-facing message about the document being expired
expectations.evidence: Lines 87–88: `case .documentExpired: message = Constants.Message.expired` where expired = "This ID has expired. Please use a valid document."

expectations.text: Has a case for .holderUnderage with a distinct user-facing message about the age requirement
expectations.evidence: Lines 89–90: `case .holderUnderage: message = Constants.Message.underage` where underage = "The document holder does not meet the age requirement (21+)."

expectations.text: Has a case for .timeout with a distinct user-facing message about the capture timing out
expectations.evidence: Lines 91–92: `case .timeout: message = Constants.Message.timeout` where timeout = "Document capture failed. Make sure the document is well lit and free of glare. Alternatively, try scanning another document"

expectations.text: Has a default case for all other rejection reasons
Does NOT use rejectHolderBelowAge as a String — it must be an Int literal (21)
expectations.evidence: Line 62: `settings.rejectHolderBelowAge = 21` — assigned as an integer literal with no quotes
